title: Public Internet Access Sites - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > Public Internet Access Sites 36 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Homeless Women Homeless Youth BROOKINGS CORE RESPONSE PEER HOUSE NAVIGATION CENTER PEER HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Navigation center offers clothing, toiletries, restrooms, light snacks, and beverages, bus passes (10 per month, by availability), internet access, device charging station, a community phone, and a temporary mailing address. Also provides a secure location for medication storage, including refrigeration and Narcan/Naloxone on site. 16389 Highway 101 S Brookings, OR 97415 Eligibility: Medication storage: Unsheltered individuals All other services: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-3pm Email (541) 251-0825 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER CASCADE YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER THE LOFT TEEN DROP-IN SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center. Offers a food pantry, help applying for state benefits such as OHP and SNAP, showers, laundry facilities. Also offers assistance with educational needs such as re-enrollment, school supplies, and specialized information and referral. Eligibility: Ages 11-24 that are homeless and unaccompanied by an adult Hours: Tuesday/Thursday noon-4pm Email (800) 660-0934 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CATHOLIC CHARITIES HOMELESS SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides drop-in services including information and referral, showers, laundry facilities, a community meal, snacks, and coffee. Offers access to a phone, community voicemail, mailing address, and computers. 2740 SE Powell Boulevard #5 Portland, OR 97202 Eligibility: Non-parenting women age 25 and older who are experiencing homelessness. Well-mannered pets are allowed. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-1pm Lunch: 11:30am-1pm Email (971) 222-1880 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHANCE 2ND CHANCE SHELTER PEER SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides services such as limited assistance obtaining Oregon ID, mailing addresses, GED tutoring, and essential supplies as available (such as toothpaste, shampoo, soap, and deodorant). Also provides job search assistance such as preparing a resume, completing job applications, and interview techniques. Other services include: counseling including anger management, detox and rehab referrals, Oregon Health Plan enrollment assistance, and computer and internet access. 1100 Jackson Street SE Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm (541) 967-8545 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DESCHUTES COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH DESCHUTES COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH THE DROP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a safe and supportive space for youth and young adults. Services include computers and wifi, charging stations for devices, games, music, movies, laundry, information on social services, coffee and snacks, and a quiet corner with activities. 1340 NW Wall Street Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Ages 14-29 Hours: Monday/Wednesday 2pm-5:30pm, Tuesday 5pm-6:30pm, Friday 1pm-4:30pm (541) 797-3623 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ECUMENICAL MINISTRIES OF OREGON ECUMENICAL MINISTRIES OF OREGON HIV DAY CENTER HIV SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center serving hot breakfast and lunches, access to phones and computers, and recreational activities such as art therapy, games, and video-based media. Also provides clothing, showers, mail services, and hygiene supplies. Guests have access to laundry services, massages, acupuncture, foot care, and haircuts on a sign-up basis. 2941 NE Ainsworth Street Basement Portland, OR 97211 Eligibility: Individuals who are HIV-positive and have incomes no greater than 300% of the federal poverty level Hours: Breakfast: Monday-Friday 9am-10:30am Lunch: Monday-Friday 1pm All other services: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 460-3822 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GOODWILL INDUSTRIES OF LANE AND SOUTH COAST COUNTIES GOODWILL WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT VETERAN CONNECTIONS HOMELESS VETERAN REINTEGRATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides employment-related services to reintegrate homeless veterans into the labor force. Services include job search training and placement, employment readiness training, career exploration, and vocational/technical training. Also provides information about community resources, including housing, transportation, identification, clothing, computer access, and more. 855 Seneca Road Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: -Must be homeless or at risk of becoming homeless, or have been homeless within 60 days prior to enrollment -Must have served at least one day of active military service and have been discharged under any status other than dishonorable Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 431-3339 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOSEA YOUTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides meals, clothing, laundry, personal hygiene items, showers, job training classes, mentoring programs, referrals to other resources, and access to telephone and computer services. For the winter season, offering tents, sleeping bags, and blankets. 834 Monroe Street Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 with a focus on individuals experiencing or at risk of homelessness Hours: Monday-Thursday 11am-6:30pm, Friday 11am-3pm Lunch: noon-2pm Dinner: 5pm Email (541) 344-5583 - Get Directions Visit Website More Details ILLINOIS VALLEY FAMILY COALITION COMPUTER LAB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers computer, printer, and internet access to members of the community to help find employment, housing, and other information and services. 535 E River Street Cave Junction, OR 97523 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4:30pm Email (541) 592-6139 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JOIN JOIN PDX Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a daytime drop-in center. Services include access to showers, bathrooms, mail pick up and PO Box setup, hygiene items, diapers, pet food, on-site WiFi and telephone usage, and specialized information and referral. 1435 NE 81st Avenue Suite 100 Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Unrestricted with priority given to homeless individuals or families Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Friday/Saturday/Sunday 9am-5pm, Tuesday/Thursday 10am-4pm. Closed on national holidays. Email (503) 232-2031 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JUST COMPASSION RESOURCE CENTER HOMELESS DAY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A day center that provides meals, a place to rest and socialize, housing resource materials, and WiFi. On-site housing liaison is available during the week. Mental health services are sometimes available on-site through partner agency. 12264 SW Hall Boulevard Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Age 18 and older experiencing homelessness Hours: Day center hours: 7 days per week 7am-7pm -Breakfast 9am-10:15am -Lunch noon-1pm -Social Services 1pm-2pm -Showers by appointment only Email (503) 624-4666 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIBRARIES OF OREGON OREGON STATE LIBRARIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Website provides a search tool to find local libraries. "Answerland" chat service can provide recommendations and other help. See individual library websites to find story times and library activities for children; search databases for articles; and download e-books, audio books, and videos. Using local libraries requires a library card. Many libraries have internet-connected computers for public use. Locator Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable Email Visit Website More Details LIFEBOAT SERVICES DAYTIME NAVIGATION SHELTER LIFEBOAT DAY SHELTER AND NAVIGATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a daytime drop-in center with access to socks, laundry facilities, Wi-Fi, computer and telephone access, fax, coffee, snacks, a clothing closet, assistance in navigating social services, and specialized information and referral. Camping gear may be available, depending on donations. 1076 Franklin Avenue Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Saturday/Sunday 6am-6pm, Thursday/Friday 8am-6pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT GRANTS PASS RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to a food pantry, personal hygiene supplies, diapers, baby formula, sleeping bags, bedding, tents, pet food, laundry facilities, and computers for schoolwork or job searching. Also offers harm reduction workshops, advocacy, case management, temporary mailing addresses, and referrals to other community resources. 223 SE M Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or a parent or guardian of school-aged children or younger in their care. Must also be currently, recently, or about to be in unstable or unsafe housing, including couch surfing, staying in hotels, camping, living on the streets, living in a car or trailer, staying in a shelter or other temporary situation, or any other unsafe or dangerous living situation. If someone does not qualify, staff will work with them to find other community resources. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 841-1974 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MASLOW PROJECT MEDFORD Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to a food pantry, personal hygiene supplies, diapers, baby formula, sleeping bags, bedding, tents, pet food, laundry facilities, and computers for schoolwork or job searching. Also offers harm reduction workshops, advocacy, case management, temporary mailing addresses, and referrals to other community resources. 500 Monroe Street Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Age 24 or younger, or a parent or guardian of school-aged children or younger in their care. Must also be currently, recently, or about to be in unstable or unsafe housing, including couch surfing, staying in hotels, camping, living on the streets, living in a car or trailer, staying in a shelter or other temporary situation, or any other unsafe or dangerous living situation. If someone does not qualify, staff will work with them to find other community resources. Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (541) 608-6868 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY COMMUNITY ACTION HOME YOUTH SERVICES HOME YOUTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a day shelter with drop-in services and an overnight emergency shelter. Supports youth with meals, laundry, showers, personal items, and community connection. Accepts school supply donations. Programs include: Runaway and Homeless Youth Program (RHY): Connects youth and their families to resources that meet their immediate needs. Also provides mediation and counseling resources for family reunification. The goal is safe exits from the streets, achieved through relationships with local law enforcement, street outreach, and working closely with the school district and other community partners. Youth Empowerment Program (YEP): Offered in 4 elements including internships and jobs, financial literacy, workshops and field trips, and extended support. Youth cohorts go through the program in 3-month cycles and can earn high school credit for their work and experiences during the program. Taylor's House is a 10 bed emergency shelter thay offers services for study support, social activities, job training. Onsite treatment and counseling services, as well as meeting rooms for appointments, visits and recreation. 625 Union Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Ages 11-17, 18 if still in school or working towards a GED Hours: The Drop In: Noon-7pm / 7 days per week Office: Monday-Friday 8am-6:30pm Email (971) 273-7300 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH BEHAVIORAL HEALTH RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a low-barrier day center that offers computers and charging stations, a temporary mailing address, laundry facilities, restrooms, showers, and resources. Access to the day center is in four, 3-hour time blocks. To enter, participants need a ticket, and 25 tickets are distributed for each time block on a first-come, first-served basis. No ticket is required to pick up mail, which is available up to four times per day. Do Good Multnomah runs shelter and bridge housing programs on site to help people continue on their path to permanent housing. Referrals come through the Day Center. 333 SW Park Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Age 18 and older, pets allowed Hours: Day Center: 7 days per week 8am-8pm (intake stops at 7:45pm), closed the 4th Friday of the month from noon-3pm Mail pick up: 9am-10am, noon-1pm, 3pm-4pm, 6pm-7pm Time Block 1: 8am-10:45am Time Block 2: 11am-1:45pm Time Block 3: 2pm-4:45pm Time Block 4: 5pm-7:45pm Email (503) 988-4100 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY LIBRARY EVENTS AND CLASSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an online community calendar to help community members find classes and events held at local libraries. Events and classes include job seeker resources (support groups and resume workshops), computer help classes and access to computers, arts and crafts instruction, book clubs and discussion groups, chess clubs, cultural celebrations and festivals, early reading skills, teen programming, lectures and author talks, money and finances programming, performances and exhibitions, story times, and special programs for parents with children ages 0-6. Eligibility: Some classes or computer lab services may require a library card Hours: Varies by class or event Email (503) 988-5123 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH DOWNTOWN DROP-IN DROP-IN DAY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community meals six days per week. Offers access to showers, laundry, clothes, computers, internet, and supportive advocates. Provides information about community resources such as education, job training, employment, housing, and LGBTQIA2S+ supports. Hosts regular activities where youth can access resources, explore their artistic talents, join recreation groups, and learn new skills. 820 SW Oak Street Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 homeless and at-risk youth with a focus on LGBTQIA2S+ youth Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-10am, 1pm-3pm, and 4pm-6:30pm, Saturday 1pm-3pm and 4pm-6:30pm. Meals served for the first hour of each shift. Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH YOUTH CONNECT DROP-IN DAY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community meals six days per week. Offers access to showers, laundry, clothes, computers, internet, and supportive advocates. Provides information about community resources such as education, job training, employment, housing, and LGBTQIA2S+ supports. Hosts regular activities where youth can access resources, explore their artistic talents, join recreation groups, and learn new skills. 43 SE 102 Avenue Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Ages 12-24 homeless and at-risk youth with a focus on LGBTQIA2S+ youth Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-10am, 1pm-3pm, and 4pm-6:30pm, Saturday 1pm-3pm and 4pm-6:30pm. Meals served for the first hour of each shift. Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEW AVENUES FOR YOUTH YOUTH OPPORTUNITY CENTER DROP-IN DAY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers access to showers, laundry, clothes, computers, internet, and supportive advocates. Hosts regular activities where youth can access resources, explore their artistic talents, join recreation groups, and learn new skills. Also connects youth to other services like education, job training, employment, housing, LGBTQIA2S+ supports, and more. 470 SE 165th Avenue Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 homeless and at-risk youth with a focus on LGBTQIA2S+ youth Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-6pm Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST HUMAN SERVICES HEALTH, OUTREACH, SHELTER, AND TRANSITIONS PROGRAM HOST RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop in center with access to clothing, bus passes, meals, phone and internet access, and specialized information and referral. 1115 Liberty Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Age 18-24 and are at risk or experiencing homelessness Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm Email (503) 588-5825 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OHRA OHRA COMMUNITY RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides housing services, case management, information and referral, mailing address, telephone, benefits and services assistance, job search-related printing, resume creation, hygiene supplies, and education opportunities. Showers and laundry are available twice a week. Each individual who showers can do a small load of laundry. 2350 Ashland Street Ashland, OR 97520 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Resource Center: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm Showers and Laundry: Tuesday/Thursday 10am-2pm. First come, first serve. Email (541) 631-2235 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTSIDE IN OUTSIDE IN DAY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A drop in center that provides basic needs services such as meals, recreation/activities, showers, laundry, phone, mailing address, and computers. Also offers a safe space for LGBTQ youth. 1132 SW 13th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 16-24 who are experiencing homelessness and are eligible to enter the Homeless Youth Continuum Hours: Drop-In: Tuesday/Thursday/Sunday 1pm-6pm Breakfast: Monday-Friday 9am-10am Lunch: Sunday-Friday 1pm-2pm Dinner: Sunday-Friday 5pm-6pm Email (503) 535-3800 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PROJECT HOMELESS CONNECT ACCESS CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides meals, showers, laundry, temporary mailing addresses, clothing provisions, cell phone charging, TriMet tickets, shelter and housing referrals, and connections to various community partners and services. 1440 SE 21st Avenue Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Unrestricted, with a focus on individuals who are homeless Hours: Center: Wednesday-Sunday 9am-5pm Meals: Breakfast 9am, sack lunches noon, hot meal 3pm Email (971) 713-3930 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RECOVERY OUTREACH COMMUNITY CENTER SALEM DROP-IN CENTER AND CONFERENCE ROOM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center for individuals experiencing mental health or addiction challenges. Offers a safe space to talk about trauma, anxiety, emotions, and other personal topics while supporting recovery. Daily activities include yoga, art, walking, games, internet access, and socializing. Monthly group outings are also available. 1190 Broadway Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Saturday/Sunday 11am-4pm Tuesday 12pm-5pm Email (503) 393-4273 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RECOVERY OUTREACH COMMUNITY CENTER SOCC DROP-IN CENTER AND CONFERENCE ROOM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center for individuals experiencing mental health or addiction challenges. Offers a safe space to talk about trauma, anxiety, emotions, and other personal topics while supporting recovery. Daily activities include yoga, art, walking, games, internet access, and socializing. Monthly group outings are also available. 280 NE Santiam Boulevard Mill City, OR 97360 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 12am-5pm Email (503) 392-4276 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RIGHT TRACK RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center offering lunch, food boxes, laundry, showers, computers with internet access, assistance with housing applications and cover letters, and temporary mailing addresses. May also provide pet food, hygiene supplies, and grab and go foods as supplies allow. 501 3rd Street La Grande, OR 97850 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Wednesday 9am-3pm, Friday 10am-2pm (541) 962-7994 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SE WORKS AND WORKSOURCE PORTLAND METRO-SE SE WORKS FOSTER WORKSOURCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides resources to help unemployed and underemployed people find jobs, connect with the right employers, get training for in-demand careers, and meet the requirements for unemployment benefits through a network of public and private partners. Also, help businesses find skilled workers, grow their workforce, and access job training and pre-layoff support services. 6401 SE Foster Road Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (503) 772-2300 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SE WORKS AND WORKSOURCE PORTLAND METRO-SE SE WORKS HAROLD WORKSOURCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides resources to help unemployed and underemployed people find jobs, connect with the right employers, get training for in-demand careers, and meet the requirements for unemployment benefits through a network of public and private partners. Also, help businesses find skilled workers, grow their workforce, and access job training and pre-layoff support services. 8044 SE Harold Street Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (503) 772-2300 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SHERWOOD FAMILY RESOURCE CENTER SHARE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides resources and support for families in need. Offers the use of computers and office equipment for job search and accessing community resources. Gives referrals to parenting classes. 21920 SW Sherwood Boulevard Sherwood School District Office Sherwood, OR 97140 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-3pm, during the school year. Summer hours by appointment only. Email (503) 825-5087 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY FIRST PLACE FAMILY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a day center with laundry, showers, internet access, mail services, clothing vouchers, on-site clothing, and hygiene supplies. Assistance in obtaining identification documents. 4060 W Amazon Drive Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Families with children under age 18 and pregnant women (proof of pregnancy required) who are experiencing homelessness Hours: 7 days per week 9am-5pm (541) 342-7728 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE FATHER'S HEART Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in day center with services that include two meals a day with a sack lunch to-go, showers by appointment (not wheelchair accessible), haircuts, computer and telephone access, blankets, clothing, shoes, and hygiene items. Referrals to resources surrounding housing, mental health, and substance abuse. Assistance with the determination of homelessness letters to obtain an Oregon ID. 603 12th Street Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Homeless adults age 18 and older and adults with families Hours: Day Center and meals: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm Showers: Monday-Friday 9am-1pm Email (503) 722-9780 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE ROSEWOOD INITIATIVE WELLNESS WEDNESDAYS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about internal programs, one-on-one resource navigation, workforce navigation (resumes, job search, and more), digital navigation (assistance with using computers or iPads), TriMet Low Income Fare signups, and a food pantry. 14127 SE Stark Street Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Wednesday 10am-3pm Email (503) 208-2562 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WASHINGTON COUNTY LAW LIBRARY LAW LIBRARY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides access to print and digital collection of statutes, case law, subjects covering state and federal jurisdictions, and historic Oregon legislative materials. The library offers computers for public use and meeting spaces. 111 NE Lincoln Street Suite 250-L Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 846-8880 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WORKSOURCE OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A network of public and private partners working together for job seekers and businesses: -Provide resources to help unemployed and underemployed individuals connect with the employers that are right for them, find the jobs they are looking for, get trained for the jobs they want, and meet unemployment benefit requirements -Ensure businesses have access to a skilled, work-ready talent pool aligned with industry needs -Provide employers with resources to strengthen their workforce and grow their business -Offer job training, career development, and pre-layoff support services Locator Salem, OR 97311 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 772-2300 Get
